About


My name is Marc Tremblay, and I'm a recent Computer Science graduate from University of Ontario Institute of Technology (now going by Ontario Tech University). My passion for software development started back in high school when I joined my school's robotics team and a youth education program about video game development called ProtoLaunch. After this, I went to UOIT for Game Development and Entrepreneurship and switched to Computer Science to gain a more focused education in software development. I've had summer positions at Red Meat Games and Blot Interactive, and have worked on a number of school and personal projects most of which are (going to be) detailed below.

RocketMaze Game


This is my submission for Project 1 of my Interactive Media course (CSCI 4160U) in university. The assignment consisted of making a 2D game in Unity and incorporating as many concepts as possible about 2D game development that we covered in class.

The game begins with your rocket ship on a refueling station at ground level and the goal is to launch into space, through a maze, while avoiding various obstacles, conserving fuel and not taking damage.


Concurrent Raytracer


This is my final project for a course I took in university, CSCI 4060U: Massively Parallel Programming. My plan was to create a simple raytracing app that could use different APIs for parallelism and compare them to each other and to a sequential version. The APIs I chose were OpenMP, POSIX Threads and OpenCL. Unfortuately, with other final projects, presentations and exams to worry about, I was not able to start on the OpenCL version in time to meet the submission deadline.

I do intend to implement the OpenCL version, as well as add functionality for loading a scene from a file, add support for multiple shapes (instead of just spheres), and make general improvements.
